title: priority boost queue depth threshold
purpose: D3.2 defines priority boost queue depth threshold for the agent-driven expert system. Platform evolution — promotion ladder, parallel queue, reuse.
scope: Owns `D3.2` only; siblings under `D3` must not duplicate this spec.
Aligns with minimal HITL: H1 plan, H2 blocker, H3 sign-off ([INTRO-1.2](INTRO-1.2-human-touchpoint-contract-h1-h2-h3.md)).
Conflicts resolve in favor of [Vision §6 — Branch D — Platform evolution plane (parallel queue)](../../full-automation-vision-and-hierarchy.md#6-branch-d-platform-evolution-plane-parallel-queue).
behavior_steps: On each pursuit wake, the conductor compares `state.platform.promotion_queue` depth to `drain_policy.boost_threshold` beside K—when depth exceeds the threshold, scheduling grants extra platform turns or elevated head priority so backlog cannot grow unbounded during heavy implement phases.
Boost is a pressure valve, not the default cadence: it complements [D3.1](D3.1-1-platform-turn-per-k-product-turns.md) K-step interleaving rather than replacing product turns on every wake.
Threshold and boost factor live in drain_policy; the conductor dual-writes current depth and boost state to state.json for operator dashboard visibility and SEC-15 audit rows.

When `next_action` is evidence-blocked on the active product goal, boost must not defer that work—[D3.3](D3.3-priority-cut-product-blocked-missing-tool.md) can suspend boost to favor enqueue-and-drain of the missing playbook, script, or catalog entry.
If boost would starve product pursuit, drain_policy is corrupt, or queue depth disagrees with state.json, pursuit halts at H2 until scheduling policy is reconciled.

Platform queue depth 0 but product blocked on missing playbook â€” D3.3 priority cut skips platform drain.
Edge case `D3.2` variant 4: verify state dual-write before continuing pursuit.
Pass 3: add regression test or evidence path specific to `D3.2`.
Pass 3: cross-link related nodes in same branch index.

**False complete:** Item marked done without artifact â†’ audit-hierarchy-depth.py re-enqueues deepen pass.
**Scope bleed:** Worker edits journal/state during planning-only expansion â†’ forbidden in vision-expansion-prompt.
**Stale design:** Upstream vision Â§ changes â†’ reconcile-stale adds deepen items for affected ids.
implementation: Scheduler in autopilot workflow: `(steps_total % K) == 0` â†’ platform turn.
playbook-keeper + script extraction skills dequeue promotion items.
Validate `D3.2` against SEC-15 release checklist and parent index links.
Document `D3.2` in parent index with verify command and release tag.
Add checklist row in SEC-15 release doc for `D3.2`.
acceptance: [ ] Named script, skill, or test path exists or is listed in SEC-15 release row
narrative: D3.2 defines priority boost queue depth threshold for the agent-driven expert system. Platform evolution — promotion ladder, parallel queue, reuse. In practice, the system on each pursuit wake, the conductor compares `state.platform.promotion_queue` depth to `drain_policy.boost_threshold` beside k—when depth exceeds the threshold, scheduling grants extra platform turns or elevated head priority so backlog cannot grow unbounded during heavy implement phases, then boost is a pressure valve, not the default cadence: it complements [d3.1](d3.1-1-platform-turn-per-k-product-turns.md) k-step interleaving rather than replacing product turns on every wake.
reader_narrative:
<!-- prose-source: agent plane-d 2026-06-28 -->

When promotion queue depth exceeds a configured threshold, priority boost accelerates platform drains—extra platform turns or elevated head priority—so backlog cannot grow unbounded during heavy implement phases. Boost is a pressure valve, not the default cadence; it complements [D3.1](D3.1-1-platform-turn-per-k-product-turns.md) rather than replacing K-step interleaving.

Threshold and boost factor live in drain_policy beside K. Boost must not defer the current project goal when next_action is evidence-blocked; [D3.3](D3.3-priority-cut-product-blocked-missing-tool.md) can temporarily suspend boost to favor enqueue-and-drain of the missing artifact class. Track depth in state.json for operator dashboard visibility and SEC-15 audit rows.
